"""Tests for PlanListService.""" from datetime import UTC, datetime from pathlib import Path import pytest from erk.core.services.plan_list_service import ( PlanListData, PlannedPRPlanListService, RealPlanListService, ) from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import ( GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, PRDetails, PullRequestInfo, WorkflowRun, ) from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime from erk_shared.plan_store.types import Plan, PlanState TEST_LOCATION = GitHubRepoLocation(root=Path("/test/repo"), repo_id=GitHubRepoId("owner", "repo")) class TestPlanListService: """Tests for PlanListService with injected fakes.""" def test_fetches_issues_with_empty_pr_linkages(self) -> None: """Service uses unified query even when no PR linkages exist.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body="Plan body", state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub(issues_data=[issue]) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) assert len(result.plans) == 1 assert result.plans[0].plan_identifier == "42" assert result.plans[0].title == "Test Plan" assert result.pr_linkages == {} def test_fetches_issues_and_pr_linkages_unified(self) -> None: """Service uses unified get_issues_with_pr_linkages for issues + PR linkages.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body="", state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) pr = PullRequestInfo( number=123, state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123", is_draft=False, title="PR Title", checks_passing=True, owner="owner", repo="repo", ) # Configure issues and pr_issue_linkages for unified query fake_github = FakeGitHub( issues_data=[issue], pr_issue_linkages={42: [pr]}, ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) # Unified path returns plans from get_issues_with_pr_linkages assert len(result.plans) == 1 assert result.plans[0].plan_identifier == "42" # PR linkages should be fetched together assert 42 in result.pr_linkages assert result.pr_linkages[42][0].number == 123 def test_empty_issues_returns_empty_data(self) -> None: """Service returns empty data when no issues match.""" fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() fake_github = FakeGitHub() service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) assert result.plans == [] assert result.pr_linkages == {} assert result.workflow_runs == {} def test_state_filter_with_unified_path(self) -> None: """Service passes state filter to unified get_issues_with_pr_linkages.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) open_issue = IssueInfo( number=1, title="Open Plan", body="", state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/1", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) closed_issue = IssueInfo( number=2, title="Closed Plan", body="", state="CLOSED", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/2", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) # Configure both issues for the unified query fake_github = FakeGitHub(issues_data=[open_issue, closed_issue]) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={1: open_issue, 2: closed_issue}) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], state="open", http_client=None, ) assert len(result.plans) == 1 assert result.plans[0].title == "Open Plan" def test_state_filter_closed(self) -> None: """Service passes state filter to unified get_issues_with_pr_linkages for closed issues.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) open_issue = IssueInfo( number=1, title="Open Plan", body="", state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/1", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) closed_issue = IssueInfo( number=2, title="Closed Plan", body="", state="CLOSED", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/2", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={1: open_issue, 2: closed_issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub(issues_data=[open_issue, closed_issue]) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], state="closed", http_client=None, ) assert len(result.plans) == 1 assert result.plans[0].title == "Closed Plan" class TestWorkflowRunFetching: """Tests for efficient workflow run fetching via GraphQL node_id batch API.""" def test_fetches_workflow_runs_by_node_id(self) -> None: """Service uses GraphQL nodes(ids: [...]) for efficient batch fetching.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) # Create issue with plan-header metadata containing node_id issue_body = """## Objective Test plan
plan-header ```yaml schema_version: '2' created_at: '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z' created_by: user123 worktree_name: feature-branch last_dispatched_run_id: '12345' last_dispatched_node_id: 'WFR_abc123' last_dispatched_at: '2024-01-15T11:00:00Z' ```
""" issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body=issue_body, state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) # Pre-configure workflow run that matches the node_id run = WorkflowRun( run_id="12345", status="completed", conclusion="success", branch="feature-branch", head_sha="abc123", display_title="42:abc123", created_at=now, ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) # Configure both issues (for unified query) and workflow_runs_by_node_id fake_github = FakeGitHub( issues_data=[issue], workflow_runs_by_node_id={"WFR_abc123": run}, ) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) # Verify workflow run was fetched and mapped to issue assert 42 in result.workflow_runs assert result.workflow_runs[42] is not None assert result.workflow_runs[42].run_id == "12345" def test_skips_workflow_fetch_when_skip_flag_set(self) -> None: """Service skips workflow fetching when skip_workflow_runs=True.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue_body = """
plan-header ```yaml schema_version: '2' last_dispatched_node_id: 'WFR_abc123' ```
""" issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body=issue_body, state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) run = WorkflowRun( run_id="12345", status="completed", conclusion="success", branch="main", head_sha="abc", ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub( issues_data=[issue], workflow_runs_by_node_id={"WFR_abc123": run}, ) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], skip_workflow_runs=True, http_client=None, ) # Workflow runs dict should be empty when skipped assert result.workflow_runs == {} def test_handles_missing_node_id_gracefully(self) -> None: """Service handles issues without node_id in body.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body="Plain body without metadata", state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub(issues_data=[issue]) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) # No workflow runs should be fetched (no node_ids to fetch) assert result.workflow_runs == {} def test_handles_node_id_not_found(self) -> None: """Service handles case where node_id not found in GraphQL results.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue_body = """
plan-header ```yaml schema_version: '2' last_dispatched_node_id: 'WFR_nonexistent' ```
""" issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body=issue_body, state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) # No workflow runs configured - node_id won't be found fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub(issues_data=[issue]) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) # Issue should have None for workflow run (not found) assert 42 in result.workflow_runs assert result.workflow_runs[42] is None def test_workflow_run_api_failure_returns_empty_runs(self) -> None: """Service continues with empty workflow runs when API fails.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) issue_body = """
plan-header ```yaml schema_version: '2' last_dispatched_node_id: 'WFR_abc123' ```
""" issue = IssueInfo( number=42, title="Test Plan", body=issue_body, state="OPEN", url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, author="test-user", ) # Configure GitHub to raise an error when fetching workflow runs fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: issue}) fake_github = FakeGitHub( issues_data=[issue], workflow_runs_error="Network unreachable", ) service = RealPlanListService(fake_github, fake_issues, time=FakeTime()) result = service.get_plan_list_data( location=TEST_LOCATION, labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], http_client=None, ) # Plans should still be returned assert len(result.plans) == 1 assert result.plans[0].plan_identifier == "42" # Workflow runs should be empty due to API failure assert result.workflow_runs == {} class TestPlanListData: """Tests for PlanListData dataclass.""" def test_dataclass_is_frozen(self) -> None: """PlanListData instances are immutable.""" data = PlanListData( plans=[], pr_linkages={}, workflow_runs={}, ) with pytest.raises(AttributeError): data.plans = [] # type: ignore[misc] -- intentionally mutating frozen dataclass to test immutability def test_dataclass_contains_all_fields(self) -> None: """PlanListData has all expected fields.""" now = datetime.now(UTC) plans = [ Plan( plan_identifier="1", title="Plan", body="", state=PlanState.OPEN, url="", labels=["erk-planned-pr", "erk-plan"], assignees=[], created_at=now, updated_at=now, metadata={"number": 1}, objective_id=None, ) ] pr = PullRequestInfo( number=10, state="OPEN", url="", is_draft=False, title="PR", checks_passing=True, owner="owner", repo="repo", ) linkages = {1: [pr]} run = WorkflowRun( run_id="100", status="completed", conclusion="success", branch="main", head_sha="abc", ) runs: dict[int, WorkflowRun | None] = {1: run} data = PlanListData( plans=plans, pr_linkages=linkages, workflow_runs=runs, api_ms=150.5, plan_parsing_ms=42.3,     72→class RealPlanDataProvider(PlanDataProvider):
    73→    """Production implementation that wraps PlanListService.
    74→
    75→    Transforms PlanListData into PlanRowData for TUI display.
    76→    """
    77→
    78→    def __init__(
    79→        self,
    80→        ctx: ErkContext,
    81→        *,
    82→        location: GitHubRepoLocation,
    83→        clipboard: Clipboard,
    84→        browser: BrowserLauncher,
    85→        http_client: HttpClient,
    86→    ) -> None:
    87→        """Initialize with context and repository info.
    88→
    89→        Args:
    90→            ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
    91→            location: GitHub repository location (local root + repo identity)
    92→            clipboard: Clipboard interface for copy operations
    93→            browser: BrowserLauncher interface for opening URLs
    94→            http_client: HTTP client for direct API calls (faster than subprocess)
    95→        """
    96→        self._ctx = ctx
    97→        self._location = location
    98→        self._clipboard = clipboard
    99→        self._browser = browser
   100→        self._http_client = http_client
   101→
   102→    @property
   103→    def repo_root(self) -> Path:
   104→        """Get the repository root path."""
   105→        return self._location.root
   106→
   107→    @property
   108→    def clipboard(self) -> Clipboard:
   109→        """Get the clipboard interface for copy operations."""
   110→        return self._clipboard
   111→
   112→    @property
   113→    def browser(self) -> BrowserLauncher:
   114→        """Get the browser launcher interface for opening URLs."""
   115→        return self._browser
   116→
   117→    def fetch_plans(self, filters: PlanFilters) -> tuple[list[PlanRowData], FetchTimings | None]:
   118→        """Fetch plans and transform to TUI row format.
   119→
   120→        Args:
   121→            filters: Filter options for the query
   122→
   123→        Returns:
   124→            Tuple of (list of PlanRowData for display, optional FetchTimings breakdown)
   125→        """
   126→        t_total_start = self._ctx.time.monotonic()
   127→
   128→        # Determine if we need workflow runs
   129→        needs_workflow_runs = filters.show_runs or filters.run_state is not None
   130→
   131→        # Route to the appropriate service based on the view's labels
   132→        # Only pass http_client when it supports direct API calls (RealHttpClient)
   133→        http_for_service = self._http_client if self._http_client.supports_direct_api else None
   134→        # Objectives have their own dedicated service; all other queries
   135→        # (plans, learn plans, custom label combos) use the plan list service.
   136→        if "erk-objective" in filters.labels:
   137→            plan_data = self._ctx.objective_list_service.get_objective_list_data(
   138→                location=self._location,
   139→                state=filters.state,
   140→                limit=filters.limit,
   141→                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
   142→                creator=filters.creator,
   143→                exclude_labels=list(filters.exclude_labels) if filters.exclude_labels else None,
   144→            )
   145→        else:
   146→            plan_data = self._ctx.plan_list_service.get_plan_list_data(
   147→                location=self._location,
   148→                labels=list(filters.labels),
   149→                state=filters.state,
   150→                limit=filters.limit,
   151→                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
   152→                creator=filters.creator,
   153→                exclude_labels=list(filters.exclude_labels) if filters.exclude_labels else None,
   154→                http_client=http_for_service,
   155→            )
   156→
   157→        # Build local worktree mapping
   158→        t_wt_start = self._ctx.time.monotonic()
   159→        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()
   160→        t_wt_end = self._ctx.time.monotonic()
   161→
   162→        # Use pre-converted Plan objects from PlanListData
   163→        plans = plan_data.plans
   164→
   165→        # Transform to PlanRowData
   166→        t_rows_start = self._ctx.time.monotonic()
   167→        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
   168→        global_config = self._ctx.global_config
   169→        use_graphite = global_config.use_graphite if global_config is not None else False
   170→
   171→        for plan in plans:
   172→            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)
   173→
   174→            # Get workflow run for filtering
   175→            workflow_run = plan_data.workflow_runs.get(plan_id)
   176→
   177→            # Apply run_state filter
   178→            if filters.run_state is not None:
   179→                if workflow_run is None:
   180→                    continue
   181→                if get_workflow_run_state(workflow_run) != filters.run_state:
   182→                    continue
   183→
   184→            # Build row data
   185→            row = self._build_row_data(
   186→                plan=plan,
   187→                plan_id=plan_id,
   188→                pr_linkages=plan_data.pr_linkages,
   189→                workflow_run=workflow_run,
   190→                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
   191→                use_graphite=use_graphite,
   192→            )
   193→            rows.append(row)
   194→        t_rows_end = self._ctx.time.monotonic()
   195→
   196→        # Build timing breakdown
   197→        # api_ms covers REST + GraphQL enrichment from PlanListData
   198→        # plan_parsing_ms covers body parsing from PlanListData
   199→        # workflow_runs_ms covers workflow run fetching from PlanListData
   200→        timings = FetchTimings(
   201→            rest_issues_ms=plan_data.api_ms,
   202→            graphql_enrich_ms=0.0,
   203→            plan_parsing_ms=plan_data.plan_parsing_ms,
   204→            workflow_runs_ms=plan_data.workflow_runs_ms,
   205→            worktree_mapping_ms=(t_wt_end - t_wt_start) * 1000,
   206→            row_building_ms=(t_rows_end - t_rows_start) * 1000,
   207→            total_ms=(t_rows_end - t_total_start) * 1000,
   208→        )
   209→
   210→        logger.info("fetch_plans timings: %s", timings.summary())
   211→
   212→        # Write timing to log file for post-execution analysis
   213→        self._append_timing_log(timings, len(rows))
   214→
   215→        return (rows, timings)
   216→
   217→    def close_plan(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> list[int]:
   218→        """Close a plan and its linked PRs using direct HTTP calls.
   219→
   220→        This method uses the HTTP client directly instead of subprocess calls
   221→        for significantly faster execution in the TUI.
   222→
   223→        Args:
   224→            plan_id: The plan ID to close
   225→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   226→
   227→        Returns:
   228→            List of PR numbers that were also closed
   229→        """
   230→        # Parse owner/repo from issue URL
   231→        owner_repo = self._parse_owner_repo_from_url(plan_url)
   232→        if owner_repo is None:
   233→            return []
   234→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   235→
   236→        # Close linked PRs first
   237→        closed_prs = self._close_linked_prs_http(plan_id, owner, repo)
   238→
   239→        # Close the plan (issue) via HTTP
   240→        self._http_client.patch(
   241→            f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{plan_id}",
   242→            data={"state": "closed"},
   243→        )
   244→
   245→        return closed_prs
   246→
   247→    def _parse_owner_repo_from_url(self, url: str) -> tuple[str, str] | None:
   248→        """Parse owner and repo from a GitHub URL.
   249→
   250→        Args:
   251→            url: GitHub URL (e.g., "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123")
   252→
   253→        Returns:
   254→            Tuple of (owner, repo) or None if parsing fails
   255→        """
   256→        # URL format: https://github.com/owner/repo/...
   257→        if not url.startswith("https://github.com/"):
   258→            return None
   259→        parts = url.split("/")
   260→        # parts: ['https:', '', 'github.com', 'owner', 'repo', ...]
   261→        if len(parts) < 5:
   262→            return None
   263→        return (parts[3], parts[4])
   264→
   265→    def _close_linked_prs_http(
   266→        self,
   267→        plan_id: int,
   268→        owner: str,
   269→        repo: str,
   270→    ) -> list[int]:
   271→        """Close all OPEN PRs linked to an issue using HTTP.
   272→
   273→        Uses the GitHub REST API via HTTP client for fast execution.
   274→
   275→        Args:
   276→            plan_id: The plan ID
   277→            owner: Repository owner
   278→            repo: Repository name
   279→
   280→        Returns:
   281→            List of PR numbers that were closed
   282→        """
   283→        # Use the existing gateway to get PR linkages (still efficient for read)
   284→        location = GitHubRepoLocation(
   285→            root=self._location.root,
   286→            repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo),
   287→        )
   288→        pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_prs_linked_to_issues(location, [plan_id])
   289→        linked_prs = pr_linkages.get(plan_id, [])
   290→
   291→        closed_prs: list[int] = []
   292→        for pr in linked_prs:
   293→            if pr.state == "OPEN":
   294→                # Close via HTTP client for speed
   295→                self._http_client.patch(
   296→                    f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr.number}",
   297→                    data={"state": "closed"},
   298→                )
   299→                closed_prs.append(pr.number)
   300→
   301→        return closed_prs
   302→
   303→    def submit_to_queue(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> None:
   304→        """Submit a plan to the implementation queue.
   305→
   306→        Runs 'erk pr dispatch' as a subprocess to handle the complex workflow
   307→        of creating branches, PRs, and triggering GitHub Actions.
   308→
   309→        Args:
   310→            plan_id: The plan ID to submit
   311→            plan_url: The plan URL (unused, kept for interface consistency)
   312→        """
   313→        # Run erk pr dispatch command from the repository root
   314→        # -f flag prevents blocking on existing branch prompts in TUI context
   315→        subprocess.run(
   316→            ["erk", "pr", "dispatch", str(plan_id), "-f"],
   317→            cwd=self._location.root,
   318→            check=True,
   319→            capture_output=True,
   320→        )
   321→
   322→    def fetch_branch_activity(self, rows: list[PlanRowData]) -> dict[int, BranchActivity]:
   323→        """Fetch branch activity for plans that exist locally.
   324→
   325→        Args:
   326→            rows: List of plan rows to fetch activity for
   327→
   328→        Returns:
   329→            Mapping of plan_id to BranchActivity for plans with local worktrees.
   330→        """
   331→        result: dict[int, BranchActivity] = {}
   332→
   333→        # Get trunk branch
   334→        trunk = self._ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(self._location.root)
   335→
   336→        for row in rows:
   337→            # Only fetch for plans with local branches
   338→            if not row.exists_locally or row.worktree_branch is None:
   339→                continue
   340→
   341→            # Get commits on branch not in trunk
   342→            commits = self._ctx.git.branch.get_branch_commits_with_authors(
   343→                self._location.root,
   344→                row.worktree_branch,
   345→                trunk,
   346→                limit=1,  # Only need most recent
   347→            )
   348→
   349→            if commits:
   350→                # Parse ISO timestamp from git
   351→                timestamp_str = commits[0]["timestamp"]
   352→                commit_at = datetime.fromisoformat(timestamp_str)
   353→                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity(
   354→                    last_commit_at=commit_at,
   355→                    last_commit_author=commits[0]["author"],
   356→                )
   357→            else:
   358→                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity.empty()
   359→
   360→        return result
   361→
   362→    def fetch_plan_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
   363→        """Fetch plan content from the first comment of an issue, or return body directly.
   364→
   365→        For issue-based plans: uses plan_comment_id from the issue body metadata to
   366→        fetch the specific comment containing the plan content.
   367→
   368→        For draft PR plans: plan_body IS the plan content (already extracted from the
   369→        PR body by PlannedPRPlanListService). No metadata block is present, so return
   370→        plan_body directly.
   371→
   372→        Args:
   373→            plan_id: The GitHub issue/PR number
   374→            plan_body: The body text — either an issue body (with plan-header metadata
   375→                and plan_comment_id) or already-extracted plan content (draft PR case)
   376→
   377→        Returns:
   378→            The extracted plan content, or None if not found
   379→        """
   380→        # Check for plan-header metadata block to distinguish plan types:
   381→        # - Draft PR plans: no metadata block; plan_body IS the extracted content
   382→        # - Issue-based plans: metadata block with plan_comment_id pointing to the comment
   383→        block = find_metadata_block(plan_body, "plan-header")
   384→        if block is None:
   385→            return plan_body if plan_body.strip() else None
   386→
   387→        comment_id = block.data.get(PLAN_COMMENT_ID)
   388→        if not isinstance(comment_id, int):
   389→            return None
   390→
   391→        # Fetch the comment via HTTP client
   392→        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
   393→        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
   394→        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"
   395→
   396→        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
   397→        comment_body = response.get("body", "")
   398→
   399→        # Extract plan content from comment
   400→        return extract_plan_from_comment(comment_body)
   401→
   402→    def fetch_objective_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
   403→        """Fetch objective content from the first comment of an issue.
   404→
   405→        Uses the objective_comment_id from the issue body metadata to fetch
   406→        the specific comment containing the objective content.
   407→
   408→        Args:
   409→            plan_id: The GitHub issue number
   410→            plan_body: The issue body (to extract objective_comment_id from metadata)
   411→
   412→        Returns:
   413→            The extracted objective content, or None if not found
   414→        """
   415→        comment_id = extract_objective_header_comment_id(plan_body)
   416→        if comment_id is None:
   417→            return None
   418→
   419→        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
   420→        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
   421→        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"
   422→
   423→        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
   424→        comment_body = response.get("body", "")
   425→
   426→        return extract_objective_from_comment(comment_body)
   427→
   428→    def fetch_plans_by_ids(self, plan_ids: set[int]) -> list[PlanRowData]:
   429→        """Fetch specific plans by their issue numbers.
   430→
   431→        Uses get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages for targeted fetching
   432→        instead of listing all plans and filtering.
   433→
   434→        Args:
   435→            plan_ids: Set of plan issue numbers to fetch
   436→
   437→        Returns:
   438→            List of PlanRowData objects sorted by plan_id
   439→        """
   440→        if not plan_ids:
   441→            return []
   442→
   443→        issues, pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages(
   444→            location=self._location,
   445→            issue_numbers=list(plan_ids),
   446→        )
   447→
   448→        # Convert IssueInfo -> Plan
   449→        plans = [issue_info_to_plan(issue) for issue in issues]
   450→
   451→        # Build worktree mapping
   452→        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()
   453→
   454→        # Build row data
   455→        global_config = self._ctx.global_config
   456→        use_graphite = global_config.use_graphite if global_config is not None else False
   457→        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
   458→        for plan in plans:
   459→            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)
   460→            row = self._build_row_data(
   461→                plan=plan,
   462→                plan_id=plan_id,
   463→                pr_linkages=pr_linkages,
   464→                workflow_run=None,
   465→                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
   466→                use_graphite=use_graphite,
   467→            )
   468→            rows.append(row)
   469→
   470→        rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.plan_id)
   471→        return rows
   472→
   473→    def fetch_plans_for_objective(self, objective_issue: int) -> list[PlanRowData]:
   474→        """Fetch plans associated with a specific objective.
   475→
   476→        Fetches erk-plan issues in both open and closed states, then filters
   477→        client-side by objective_issue.
   478→
   479→        Args:
   480→            objective_issue: The objective issue number to filter by
   481→
   482→        Returns:
   483→            List of PlanRowData objects for plans linked to this objective
   484→        """
   485→        all_plans: list[PlanRowData] = []
   486→        for state in ("open", "closed"):
   487→            filters = PlanFilters(
   488→                labels=("erk-plan",),
   489→                state=state,
   490→                run_state=None,
   491→                limit=100,
   492→                show_prs=True,
   493→                show_runs=False,
   494→                exclude_labels=(),
   495→                creator=None,
   496→            )
   497→            rows, _timings = self.fetch_plans(filters)
   498→            all_plans.extend(rows)
   499→        return [row for row in all_plans if row.objective_issue == objective_issue]
   500→
   501→    def fetch_unresolved_comments(self, pr_number: int) -> list[PRReviewThread]:
   502→        """Fetch unresolved review threads for a pull request.

   504→        Args:

   506→
   507→        Returns:

   509→        """
   510→        return self._ctx.github.get_pr_review_threads(

   514→    def _append_timing_log(self, timings: FetchTimings, row_count: int) -> None:
   515→        """Append timing data to .erk/scratch/dash-timings.log for post-execution analysis.
   516→
   517→        Silently ignores all errors (sentinel paths in tests, missing directory,
   518→        permissions, etc.) since timing logs are strictly informational.
   519→
   520→        Args:
   521→            timings: Timing breakdown for this fetch cycle
   522→            row_count: Number of rows returned
   523→        """
   524→        try:
   525→            log_dir = self._location.root / ".erk" / "scratch"
   526→            if not log_dir.is_dir():
   527→                return
   528→            log_file = log_dir / "dash-timings.log"
   529→            timestamp = self._ctx.time.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
   530→            line = f"{timestamp}  rows={row_count}  {timings.summary()}\n"
   531→            with open(log_file, "a") as f:
   532→                f.write(line)
   533→        except Exception:
   534→            logger.debug("Failed to write timing log", exc_info=True)
   535→
   536→    def _build_worktree_mapping(self) -> dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]]:
   537→        """Build mapping of plan ID to (worktree name, branch).
   538→
   539→        Reads .impl/plan-ref.json from each worktree to discover the plan ID.
   540→        Plan-ref.json is the sole source of truth for plan-to-branch mapping.
   541→
   542→        Returns:
   543→            Mapping of plan ID to tuple of (worktree_name, branch_name)
   544→        """
   545→        _ensure_erk_metadata_dir_from_context(self._ctx.repo)
   546→        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]] = {}
   547→        worktrees = self._ctx.git.worktree.list_worktrees(self._location.root)
   548→        for worktree in worktrees:
   549→            impl_dir = worktree.path / ".impl"
   550→            plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
   551→            if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
   552→                issue_number = int(plan_ref.plan_id)
   553→                if issue_number not in worktree_by_plan_id:
   554→                    worktree_by_plan_id[issue_number] = (
   555→                        worktree.path.name,
   556→                        worktree.branch,
   557→                    )
   558→        return worktree_by_plan_id
   559→
   560→    def _build_row_data(
   561→        self,
   562→        *,
   563→        plan: Plan,
   564→        plan_id: int,
   565→        pr_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]],
   566→        workflow_run: WorkflowRun | None,
   567→        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]],
   568→        use_graphite: bool,
   569→    ) -> PlanRowData:
   570→        """Build a single PlanRowData from plan and related data."""
   571→        full_title = plan.title
   572→
   573→        # Worktree info
   574→        worktree_name = ""
   575→        worktree_branch: str | None = None
   576→        exists_locally = False
   577→
   578→        if plan_id in worktree_by_plan_id:
   579→            worktree_name, worktree_branch = worktree_by_plan_id[plan_id]
   580→            exists_locally = True
   581→
   582→        # Extract from pre-parsed header fields (no repeated YAML parsing)
   583→        local_impl_str: str | None = None
   584→        remote_impl_str: str | None = None
   585→        learn_status: str | None = None
   586→        learn_plan_issue: int | None = None
   587→        learn_plan_pr: int | None = None
   588→        learn_run_id: str | None = None
   589→        if plan.header_fields:
   590→            extracted = header_str(plan.header_fields, WORKTREE_NAME)
   591→            if extracted and not worktree_name:
   592→                worktree_name = extracted
   593→            local_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
   594→            remote_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)
   595→            learn_status = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_STATUS)
   596→            learn_plan_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE)
   597→            learn_plan_pr = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_PR)
   598→            learn_run_id = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_RUN_ID)
   599→
   600→        # Extract objective_issue from pre-parsed header fields
   601→        objective_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, OBJECTIVE_ISSUE)
   602→
   603→        # Learn plan issue closed state is not fetched (too slow for N sequential calls).
   604→        # Falls back to open icon (📋) which is acceptable.
   605→        learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None = None
   606→
   607→        # Format learn display (full text for detail modal, icon-only for table)
   608→        learn_display = _format_learn_display(
   609→            learn_status,
   610→            learn_plan_issue,
   611→            learn_plan_pr,
   612→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   613→        )
   614→        learn_display_icon = _format_learn_display_icon(
   615→            learn_status,
   616→            learn_plan_issue,
   617→            learn_plan_pr,
   618→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   619→        )
   620→
   621→        # Get datetime versions for storage (already parsed by YAML)
   622→        last_local_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
   623→        last_remote_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)
   624→
   625→        # Format time displays
   626→        local_impl = format_relative_time(local_impl_str)
   627→        local_impl_display = local_impl if local_impl else "-"
   628→        remote_impl = format_relative_time(remote_impl_str)
   629→        remote_impl_display = remote_impl if remote_impl else "-"
   630→
   631→        # PR info
   632→        pr_number: int | None = None
   633→        pr_url: str | None = None
   634→        pr_title: str | None = None
   635→        pr_state: str | None = None
   636→        pr_head_branch: str | None = None
   637→        pr_display = "-"
   638→        checks_display = "-"
   639→
   640→        # Comment counts - "-" when no PR
   641→        resolved_comment_count = 0
   642→        total_comment_count = 0
   643→        comments_display = "-"
   644→
   645→        # PR status fields for lifecycle enrichment
   646→        pr_is_draft: bool | None = None
   647→        pr_has_conflicts: bool | None = None
   648→        pr_review_decision: str | None = None
   649→        pr_checks_passing: bool | None = None
   650→        pr_has_unresolved_comments: bool | None = None
   651→        pr_is_stacked: bool | None = None
   652→
   653→        if plan_id in pr_linkages:
   654→            issue_prs = pr_linkages[plan_id]
   655→            selected_pr = select_display_pr(issue_prs, exclude_pr_numbers=None)
   656→            if selected_pr is not None:
   657→                pr_number = selected_pr.number
   658→                pr_title = selected_pr.title
   659→                pr_state = selected_pr.state
   660→                pr_head_branch = selected_pr.head_branch
   661→                graphite_url = self._ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(
   662→                    GitHubRepoId(selected_pr.owner, selected_pr.repo), selected_pr.number
   663→                )
   664→                pr_url = graphite_url if use_graphite and graphite_url else selected_pr.url
   665→                emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(selected_pr)
   666→                if selected_pr.will_close_target:
   667→                    emoji += "🔗"
   668→                pr_display = f"#{selected_pr.number} {emoji}"
   669→                checks_display = format_checks_cell(selected_pr)
   670→
   671→                # Extract status fields for lifecycle enrichment
   672→                pr_is_draft = selected_pr.is_draft
   673→                pr_has_conflicts = selected_pr.has_conflicts
   674→                pr_review_decision = selected_pr.review_decision
   675→                pr_checks_passing = selected_pr.checks_passing
   676→                if selected_pr.base_ref_name is not None:
   677→                    pr_is_stacked = selected_pr.base_ref_name not in ("master", "main")
   678→
   679→                # Supplement with Graphite's local stack tracking — plan PRs
   680→                # always target master on GitHub even when Graphite-stacked
   681→                if pr_is_stacked is not True and pr_head_branch is not None:
   682→                    parent = self._ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(
   683→                        self._location.root, pr_head_branch
   684→                    )
   685→                    if parent is not None and parent not in ("master", "main"):
   686→                        pr_is_stacked = True
   687→
   688→                # Get review thread counts from batched PR data
   689→                if selected_pr.review_thread_counts is not None:
   690→                    resolved_comment_count, total_comment_count = selected_pr.review_thread_counts
   691→                    comments_display = f"{resolved_comment_count}/{total_comment_count}"
   692→                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = total_comment_count > resolved_comment_count
   693→                else:
   694→                    comments_display = "0/0"
   695→                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = False
   696→
   697→        # Workflow run info
   698→        run_id: str | None = None
   699→        run_status: str | None = None
   700→        run_conclusion: str | None = None
   701→        run_id_display = "-"
   702→        run_state_display = "-"
   703→        run_url: str | None = None
   704→
   705→        if workflow_run is not None:
   706→            run_id = str(workflow_run.run_id)
   707→            run_status = workflow_run.status
   708→            run_conclusion = workflow_run.conclusion
   709→            if plan.url:
   710→                parts = plan.url.split("/")
   711→                if len(parts) >= 5:
   712→                    owner = parts[-4]
   713→                    repo_name = parts[-3]
   714→                    run_url = (
   715→                        f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{workflow_run.run_id}"
   716→                    )
   717→            run_id_display = format_workflow_run_id(workflow_run, run_url)
   718→            run_state_display = format_workflow_outcome(workflow_run)
   719→
   720→        # Log entries (empty for now - will be fetched on demand in the modal)
   721→        log_entries: tuple[tuple[str, str, str], ...] = ()
   722→
   723→        # Build learn_run_url for pending status
   724→        learn_run_url: str | None = None
   725→        if learn_run_id is not None and plan.url is not None:
   726→            parts = plan.url.split("/")
   727→            if len(parts) >= 5:
   728→                owner = parts[-4]
   729→                repo_name = parts[-3]
   730→                learn_run_url = (
   731→                    f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{learn_run_id}"
   732→                )
   733→
   734→        # Format objective display
   735→        objective_url = (
   736→            f"https://github.com/{self._location.repo_id.owner}/{self._location.repo_id.repo}/issues/{objective_issue}"
   737→            if objective_issue is not None
   738→            else None
   739→        )
   740→        objective_display = f"#{objective_issue}" if objective_issue is not None else "-"
   741→
   742→        # Compute slug display
   743→        objective_slug_display = "-"
   744→        if plan.body:
   745→            slug = extract_objective_slug(plan.body)
   746→            if slug is not None:
   747→                objective_slug_display = slug[:25]
   748→            elif plan.title:
   749→                stripped = plan.title.removeprefix("Objective: ")
   750→                objective_slug_display = stripped[:25]
   751→
   752→        # Parse roadmap for objective-specific fields
   753→        objective_done_nodes = 0
   754→        objective_total_nodes = 0
   755→        objective_progress_display = "-"
   756→        objective_state_display = "-"
   757→        objective_head_state = "-"
   758→        objective_next_node_display = "-"
   759→        objective_head_plans: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   760→        if plan.body:
   761→            phases, _errors = parse_roadmap(plan.body)
   762→            if phases:
   763→                graph = build_graph(phases)
   764→                summary = compute_graph_summary(graph)
   765→                objective_done_nodes = summary["done"]
   766→                objective_total_nodes = summary["total_nodes"]
   767→                objective_progress_display = f"{objective_done_nodes}/{objective_total_nodes}"
   768→                objective_state_display = build_state_sparkline(graph.nodes)
   769→                next_node = find_graph_next_node(graph, phases)
   770→                if next_node is not None:
   771→                    objective_next_node_display = next_node["id"]
   772→                    node_status = next_node.get("status")
   773→                    min_status = graph.min_dep_status(next_node["id"])
   774→                    if node_status == "planning":
   775→                        objective_head_state = "planning"
   776→                    elif min_status is None or min_status in _TERMINAL_STATUSES:
   777→                        objective_head_state = "ready"
   778→                    else:
   779→                        objective_head_state = min_status.replace("_", " ")
   780→
   781→                    # Collect blocking dep PR numbers for the next node
   782→                    target = next((n for n in graph.nodes if n.id == next_node["id"]), None)
   783→                    if target is not None and target.depends_on:
   784→                        node_map = {n.id: n for n in graph.nodes}
   785→                        for dep_id in target.depends_on:
   786→                            if dep_id in node_map:
   787→                                dep = node_map[dep_id]
   788→                                if dep.status not in _TERMINAL_STATUSES and dep.pr is not None:
   789→                                    num = dep.pr.lstrip("#")
   790→                                    repo_id = self._location.repo_id
   791→                                    url = f"https://github.com/{repo_id.owner}/{repo_id.repo}/issues/{num}"
   792→                                    objective_head_plans.append((dep.pr, url))
   793→
   794→        # Format updated_at display
   795→        updated_display = format_relative_time(plan.updated_at.isoformat()) or "-"
   796→
   797→        # Format created_at display
   798→        created_display = format_relative_time(plan.created_at.isoformat()) or "-"
   799→
   800→        # Determine if this is a learn plan
   801→        is_learn_plan = "erk-learn" in plan.labels
   802→
   803→        # Compute lifecycle display from header or infer from metadata
   804→        lifecycle_display = _compute_lifecycle_display(
   805→            plan, has_workflow_run=workflow_run is not None
   806→        )
   807→
   808→        # Compute status indicators separately for the "sts" column
   809→        status_display = compute_status_indicators(
   810→            lifecycle_display,
   811→            is_draft=pr_is_draft,
   812→            has_conflicts=pr_has_conflicts,
   813→            review_decision=pr_review_decision,
   814→            checks_passing=pr_checks_passing,
   815→            has_unresolved_comments=pr_has_unresolved_comments,
   816→            is_stacked=pr_is_stacked,
   817→        )
   818→
   819→        return PlanRowData(
   820→            plan_id=plan_id,
   821→            plan_url=plan.url,
   822→            pr_number=pr_number,
   823→            pr_url=pr_url,
   824→            pr_display=pr_display,
   825→            checks_display=checks_display,
   826→            worktree_name=worktree_name,
   827→            exists_locally=exists_locally,
   828→            local_impl_display=local_impl_display,
   829→            remote_impl_display=remote_impl_display,
   830→            run_id_display=run_id_display,
   831→            run_state_display=run_state_display,
   832→            run_url=run_url,
   833→            full_title=full_title,
   834→            plan_body=plan.body or "",
   835→            pr_title=pr_title,
   836→            pr_state=pr_state,
   837→            pr_head_branch=pr_head_branch,
   838→            worktree_branch=worktree_branch,
   839→            last_local_impl_at=last_local_impl_at,
   840→            last_remote_impl_at=last_remote_impl_at,
   841→            run_id=run_id,

   846→            total_comment_count=total_comment_count,

   849→            learn_plan_issue=learn_plan_issue,
   850→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   851→            learn_plan_pr=learn_plan_pr,
   852→            learn_run_url=learn_run_url,
   853→            learn_display=learn_display,
   854→            learn_display_icon=learn_display_icon,
   855→            objective_issue=objective_issue,
   856→            objective_url=objective_url,
   857→            objective_display=objective_display,
   858→            objective_done_nodes=objective_done_nodes,
   859→            objective_total_nodes=objective_total_nodes,
   860→            objective_progress_display=objective_progress_display,
   861→            objective_slug_display=objective_slug_display,
   862→            objective_state_display=objective_state_display,
   863→            objective_head_state=objective_head_state,
   864→            objective_head_plans=tuple(objective_head_plans),
   865→            objective_next_node_display=objective_next_node_display,

   867→            updated_display=updated_display,
   868→            created_at=plan.created_at,
   869→            created_display=created_display,
   870→            author=str(plan.metadata.get("author", "")),

   874→        )
   875→
   876→
   877→def _format_learn_display(
   878→    learn_status: str | None,
   879→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
   880→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
   881→    *,
   882→    learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None,
   883→) -> str:
   884→    """Format learn status for display with inline descriptions.
   885→
   886→    Args:
   887→        learn_status: Raw status value from plan header
   888→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of generated learn plan
   889→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
   890→        learn_plan_issue_closed: Whether the learn plan issue is closed
   891→
   892→    Returns:
   893→        Formatted display string based on status:
   894→        - None or "not_started" -> "- not started"
   895→        - "pending" -> "⟳ in progress"
   896→        - "completed_no_plan" -> "∅ no insights"
   897→        - "completed_with_plan" + closed -> "✅ #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   898→        - "completed_with_plan" + open -> "📋 #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   899→        - "pending_review" -> "🚧 #789" (using learn_plan_pr for draft PR)
   900→        - "plan_completed" -> "✓ #12" (using learn_plan_pr)
   901→    """
   902→    if learn_status is None or learn_status == "not_started":
   903→        return "- not started"
   904→    if learn_status == "pending":
   905→        return "⟳ in progress"
   906→    if learn_status == "completed_no_plan":
   907→        return "∅ no insights"
   908→    if learn_status == "completed_with_plan" and learn_plan_issue is not None:
   909→        if learn_plan_issue_closed is True:
   910→            return f"✅ #{learn_plan_issue}"
   911→        return f"📋 #{learn_plan_issue}"
   912→    if learn_status == "pending_review" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   913→        return f"🚧 #{learn_plan_pr}"
   914→    if learn_status == "plan_completed" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   915→        return f"✓ #{learn_plan_pr}"
   916→    # Fallback for unknown status
   917→    return "- not started"
   918→
   919→
   920→def _format_learn_display_icon(
   921→    learn_status: str | None,
   922→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
   923→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
   924→    *,
   925→    learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None,
   926→) -> str:
   927→    """Format learn status as icon-only for table display.
   928→
   929→    Args:
   930→        learn_status: Raw status value from plan header
   931→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of generated learn plan
   932→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
   933→        learn_plan_issue_closed: Whether the learn plan issue is closed
   934→
   935→    Returns:
   936→        Icon-only display string based on status:
   937→        - None or "not_started" -> "-"
   938→        - "pending" -> "⟳"
   939→        - "completed_no_plan" -> "∅"
   940→        - "completed_with_plan" + closed -> "✅ #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   941→        - "completed_with_plan" + open -> "📋 #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   942→        - "pending_review" -> "🚧 #789" (using learn_plan_pr for draft PR)
   943→        - "plan_completed" -> "✓ #12" (using learn_plan_pr)
   944→    """
   945→    if learn_status is None or learn_status == "not_started":
   946→        return "-"
   947→    if learn_status == "pending":
   948→        return "⟳"
   949→    if learn_status == "completed_no_plan":
   950→        return "∅"
   951→    if learn_status == "completed_with_plan" and learn_plan_issue is not None:
   952→        if learn_plan_issue_closed is True:
   953→            return f"✅ #{learn_plan_issue}"
   954→        return f"📋 #{learn_plan_issue}"
   955→    if learn_status == "pending_review" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   956→        return f"🚧 #{learn_plan_pr}"
   957→    if learn_status == "plan_completed" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   958→        return f"✓ #{learn_plan_pr}"
   959→    # Fallback for unknown status
   960→    return "-"
   961→
   962→
   963→def _compute_lifecycle_display(plan: Plan, *, has_workflow_run: bool) -> str:
   964→    """Compute lifecycle stage display string for a plan.
   965→
   966→    Delegates to lifecycle.compute_lifecycle_display. This wrapper preserves
   967→    the module-private name used by callers within this file.
   968→    """
   969→    from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.lifecycle import compute_lifecycle_display
   970→
   971→    return compute_lifecycle_display(plan, has_workflow_run=has_workflow_run)
   972→
   973→
   974→def _ensure_erk_metadata_dir_from_context(repo: RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel) -> None:
   975→    """Ensure erk metadata directory exists, handling sentinel case."""
   976→    if isinstance(repo, RepoContext):
   977→        ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
